place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Anchorvale Village Hawker Centre
review_text:
This is the Mumchi stall at Anchorvale Hawker Centre. I just bought 2 coconut munchi pancakes last week and today the stall called out to me again. This time round it is the red bean flavour and yes, 2 pieces.

A friendly staff who saw my glassware containing my dinner and he put it in a plastic bag automatically without asking as he was concerned that if it slipped, I may be injured or the glass splinters would injure those around me.

I appreciate his gesture as he is sincere about his customers and his friendly demeanor will always bring a smile to my face. While customer can be taught, sincerity is demonstrated. Looking forward to my next purchase.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Anchorvale Village Hawker Centre
review_text: Great service. Staff is always friendly. She also advise us accordingly on the available flavours and waiting time. Always happy going to this outlet. The pancakes are also very tasty even the kids being picky eaters love them.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Anchorvale Village Hawker Centre
review_text: Absolutely love their pancakes here! The pancakes were soft, fluffy, and packed with tasty flavours. What made the experience even better was the owner was super friendly, welcoming, and genuinely enjoys interacting with customers. You can really feel the passion and positive vibes while waiting for your food. Highly recommended if you’re craving a delicious sweet treat with great service!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Anchorvale Village Hawker Centre
review_text: Friendly staff helped to heat up without asking. But the kueh seems a little undercook, and the starch taste is a little strong. The fillings all fall off when the staff pack it for me. Not as nice as last time when they just had it at Yishun

review_text: Love this park. Scenic. Bridges. Ponds. Chickens. Parrots. After a jog/walk, hop in to Tenderbest for a cuppa. Bliss.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This is my first time at Ci Yuan Hawker Centre, and I visited on a Sunday evening at 8 pm, which is a public holiday. As a result, most stalls were closed or closing. Despite that, I was impressed by the variety of food options available here.

I look forward to returning another time to explore more!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This is a newer hawker centre located on the ground floor of Ci Yuan Community Club. It’s cleaner & more organised than the older ones, with a good mix of food choices without too many repeats. Prices are still affordable & the overall atmosphere is relaxed - less crowded & not touristy.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I don’t live nearby so I hardly eat at Ci Yuan hawker center. Today I’m in the mood to eat some “unhealthy” kind of foods that I normally skip. So I had hokkien noodle (01-16), a duck drumstick (01-12) and fried cabbage (01-11).  Finish my lunch with chendol. I enjoyed myself. Please try out these food. My best experience so far eating at Ci Yuan hawker center. I find that the food is getting better over the years. Recommended 💕

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Newly open stall "文哥潮州鱼•蒸好吃 (no English name) yet.

Tried item C: Bitter Gourd with Egg soup ($4) + Dry Hor Fun ($1.50)
= $5.50 (Total) reasonable price.

Hor Fun (dry) with black sauce (mild sweetness), fried shallots, spring onions, mini pork lards (maybe chopped very small pc - you can ask for more if you like).
You can add more chilli paste or chopped chilli padi if you like more spicy kick 🔥 🥵  Shiok & Sweat! 💦

Bitter Gourd Soup MUST TRY!
Really generous ingredients like:
- thick & tender pork slices (maybe pork collar)
- minced pork (no fats surprisingly)
- pork balls (texture not hard when bite)
- brown strips (can someone enlighten me what it call?) thanks.
- bitter gourd not bitter (I'm a person who don't fancy bitter gourd, but it really hit my taste buds - good try for me!)

This soup really warms my heart and makes me feel missing my mum's hot soup...wow!

Met a friend there who also recommended me to try their Salted fish & peanuts pork patty & Vinegar Pork Trotters.

So I will come back again to try & share with you all again!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Clean, spacious and airy hawker center with a variety of food options. We had taliwang nasi lemak, chef wang fried rice, ccf and the sliced fish pao fan from a zi char stall.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: My First Skool at 51 Fernvale Link
review_text:
Despite having dedicated teachers for each class, the teachers work together as a collective, creating a kampong spirit within the centre that we truly love. We hope our child will embrace this same spirit as she grows.

We’re especially grateful to the teachers of Patience 1, He Laoshi, Teacher Aliyah and Teacher Ann, for their care and guidance towards our little one. We can see that our child feels comfortable and safe with her teachers. She has built a close bond with them, often talking about them and even imitating them at home.

Since she started school, we have witnessed tremendous growth in her confidence, independence and ability to express herself. It has been heartening to see how much she has grown under the care of her teachers.

As parents, we are kept closely updated on our child’s development. place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The Star Vista is one of the more spacious and pleasant malls in the Buona Vista area. I like the open layout, high ceilings, wide walkways, and the large central atrium, which makes the mall feel airy and less cramped compared with many other shopping centres

There is a good variety of restaurants and cafes here, ranging from casual meals to dessert places and family-friendly dining options. The recently opened Yo-Chi also seems very popular, with a long queue and lively crowd, adding more buzz to the mall

Another plus point is The Star Performing Arts Centre, which makes the place more than just a regular mall. It is a convenient spot for meals before or after events, and the overall environment feels clean, spacious and comfortable

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Right next to the Buona Vista MRT station.
Convenient, airy ,spacious with 3 solid floors of eateries, cafes ,restaurants & supermarket.
If you are a owner of a weak mind you will die of over eating here !
In Chinese they say 多元化选择性障碍症 !

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Christmas season is here again!
Children would think of Santa Claus, Christmas tree, snowman, Christmas gifts...
Christmas is about the birth of Jesus Christ, born in a wooden stable. But we often see a different scene. A very cooling beautiful white Christmas.
Still, this seaon is a good time to bond, to love, to share...
My family,relatives and I feasted in 'The Star' at Bouna Vista. Good food, good atmosphere, good environment
see the snow falling together. Wow! Great feeling.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Star Vista: An architectural landmark with a unique layout.

I visited the Star Vista at 2 PM on a weekend, and the mall was lively and crowded with people. From a distance, the building is very impressive with its bold, highlighted design.

The Layout & Shops:
Once inside, the layout of the stalls feels a bit more "legacy" compared to the modern exterior. There is a large staircase on the first floor which is a great spot to sit and rest for a while. The mall has a very diverse range of shops—from a Christian bookstore to a specialty bakery ingredient store.

The Location:
It is very conveniently located near the MRT station.

The Verdict:
While the interior layout is a bit unique, the building itself is stunning. It is definitely worth a visit if you are passing by the area!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors

The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. The park was however crowded in the evening with people exercising, cycling. walking their dogs and having picnics. Love the trees.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
It's a mind-blowing park for spending time with nature ,any time can go and spend time in peace , best place for morning time exercises and walking or running , meditation etc etc . Early in the morning the feeling here is very nice and quiet , the public toilet is also here ,
One wedding machine is also there to take your favourite drinks .

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Visited this community club for a swim on a Tuesday and it was a really pleasant experience. The crowd was minimal, which made the whole session feel relaxed and unhurried. There are two pools available—one indoor and one outdoor—so you can choose depending on your preference or weather. There’s also a feature pool that has just been renovated and recently reopened, and it looks clean and refreshed. Overall, it’s a nice, well-maintained spot for a quiet swim without the usual crowd. There are lifeguards on duty to keep us safe!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This swimming complex have slides and water features
2 large full length swimming pool. One of the pool is under shelter.
Locker is available for valuable item storage.
Parking is available for car, motocycle,  Bicycle.
Next to food court and there are food available together at the sport complex.
There will be swimming classes and may be crowded some of the time.
Limited table and chair around the pool while there is a stand next to the open pool.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This swimming pool finally reopened after more than 3 years of closure.

It now has 5 types of slide rides, with Rainbow dash being the only multi-slide ride. They are all MUCH milder than the ones in Wild Wild Wet.

The lifeguards are pretty strict about height limits:

Purple slide (Cotton Candy Bowl) - 1.3m height limit

orange slide (Sugar Rush) - 1.3m height limit

Green slide (Jelly Lime Loops) - 1.2m height limit

Pink slide (Jellybears Swiggle) - 1.2m height limit

The lifeguards are also very particular about wearing of "hard objects", even watches by adults/parents. Yet, ironically, I see authorized influencers being allowed to wear GoPro cameras!!

There are mini paddle boats for children 4yo and above, with minimum weight limit 30 kg, and requires adult supervision at all times.

Water guns are NOT Allowed !!

Toilets are clean.

There's no more food eatery at the balcony area near the entrance.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The swimming complex is quite big and has 4 adult water slides. Note that jewellery such as earrings or necklaces are not allowed, and swimsuits with zippers are prohibited. Kids need to be at least 120–130 cm tall to use the slides.

It gets very crowded on weekends, especially at the kids’ water play area. The adult slides were fine, just some queuing, especially for the longer slide. The Sugar Rush slide was really fast and fun.

The biggest downside is the changing/shower rooms. Around 5:30 PM, the queue gets extremely long, so it’s better to go earlier if you want to avoid the wait.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: It was a super bad experience at the sliding pool. My two girls was not allowed to take the slide just because their swimming wear got a zipper at the front. Going down slides shld be by sitting and sliding down. How in the world the front zipper affect the slide down process. Really disappointed with the system enforced. Really disappoint the kids on their school holiday.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Had a wonderful experience at Munchi Pancakes Sengkang! A big thank you to Eileen and Chau for their excellent service. They were both so friendly, welcoming, and attentive, making the whole experience even more enjoyable. The pancakes were freshly made, delicious, and prepared with care. It’s always nice to be served by staff who genuinely make customers feel valued. Highly recommend this outlet, and I’ll definitely be coming back again. Thank you, Eileen and Chau!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I'm a huge fan of their pancake! Staff is friendly with a smile. Fillings - they're generous and consistently good. I even saw the staff carefully weighing out the ingredients. Plus, if you want your pancake warmed up, all you have to do is ask. It's those little touches that show they really care ❤️❤️❤️❤️.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: This stall surprised me with very delicious and rich selection of pancakes n munchi, the stall owner offers heating up the pancakes to make it even nicer. Price friendly and suggest using fair price apps for payment to enjoy additional 10% discount 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Got 3 flavours, original black sesame, original hazelnut and charcoal peanut. Fillings were generous and rich, pancake was spongy and dense. Recommend eating it asap though.

Friendly and cheerful service is always the cherry on top.

Two thumbs up!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Stall owner was a very bubbly and polite person! Heard good things from my colleagues on the munchi pancake and definitely was up to standard. Will buy again!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
This mall has everything you need for a relaxing weekend. There is a kids’ play area, as well as a jogging track with a beautiful lake view.

It offers plenty of dining options, including Din Tai Fung, Song Fa Bak Kut Teh, and many others. For tea lovers, there are popular choices such as CHICHA San Chen and CHAGEE. If you’re looking for dessert, Henri Charpentier and Mister Donut are great options. Coffee lovers can also grab a drink at Starbucks.

The mall also has a supermarket, making it a convenient one-stop destination for shopping, dining, and leisure. It’s a perfect place to spend the weekend, especially if you prefer not to travel to the city centre.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Waterway Point is a large and spacious mall with a good mix of shops, products, services and plenty of food options. It covers most day-to-day needs and works well as a one-stop destination for residents in the area. That said, it gets very crowded on weekends, so be prepared for heavy foot traffic. On the plus side, there’s ample parking available, which helps. Overall, a solid and well-planned mall — just better enjoyed during off-peak hours.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Waterway point shopping mall shopping I would say clothing shops like Uniqlo n playdress.there is a popular bookstore n a toys r us.there is a miniso ...Japan home n value shop I would not really put it in a shopping category... More of home maybe ... waterway point for shopping not exactly a place .... For food I would not say that I'm so satisfied with waterway point's food options .... There r foods like fast foods like McDonalds, Jollibee,mos burger, n there r foods like kaya toast n munching snacks like old chang kee n fried banana.there is a foodcourt within the mall but NOT HAPPY WITH IT.there r foods like fish soup n yong Tau foo but there is no Indian food or Muslim food.there is a western stall in the foodcourt but I don't find much difference in eating fast foods n eating a western meal.There r also a Thai food stall which sells fried noodles n fried rice ... This foodcourt mainly Chinese foods ... For a Indian girl like me food options r very limited not only in this food court but I m talking about the whole waterway point mall ... I mean seriously the mall is so big n spacious but there is no any Indian food in it or at least a Muslim food on it ... Very disappointed ☹️ whenever I go waterway point the only options I get to eat is either fast food or Milo toast ...there r restaurants like buddy hoggies n han's ... But seriously if I want to enjoy a nice meal I have to spend so much money??? Can't I get to enjoy a nice meal for a cheaper price at a normal foodcourt???  There r take away shops like stuff'd .... Kebab, burrito, burrito bowl used to cost much cheaper than the price is costing now ... Just increased the price for kebab, burrito n burrito bowls ... N no dine in options from last time till now ... I felt not worth buying from this shop ... But I always c a long queue in this shop ... ☹️ There is a shop which similar like stuff'd...
Guzman y gormez which also sells kebab n burritos ... it has dine in options... BUT VERY EXPENSIVE... have tried there before ... Did not went back to that shop again 🤫 ... Oh n I just remembered... Waterway point has waffles.... For any1 who is a sweet tooth ... 😁 Many flavored drinks like luckin coffee ... N boost ... There r many flavored drinks shops in waterway point but not healthy... Too much sugar... I don't really encourage people to drink all this flavored drinks...
Whatever age anybody is or whether people with diabetes or not too much sugar intake n too much fast foods intake NOT HEALTHY...
Another thing which I want to tell everyone is that when eating at McDonalds please do not give your hp to any1 in the McDonald's... I have this prob with McDonalds whenever I m eating McDonalds... Since I eat at waterway McDonalds today n this prob happened again I wish to tell everyone... The person who is wearing the yellow shirt in the McDonald's I guess she is the manager's assistant she asked for my phone to do McDonalds feedback...I did not want to be rude so gave hery HP ... Customers r supposed to do the feedback not any1 else ... The McDonald's was so dirty ... I had to wipe the table before I eat ... Fly was flying around... I want to feedback but cannot bec she do feedback... The feedback she did from my phone was submitted under my name but I did do the feedback... Just bec they want to increase their outlet rating they do feedback from customers phone ... Everything they put 5 star ... Taking a customer's phone n doing feedback is very wrong ... As a customer I can make a complaint as this has happened many times ... But I just decided to just leave it ... Next time I go McDonalds when again ask for my phone I m strictly going to say as a customer I will do feedback ... I guess I have to be rude at times ... M being too nice ... Peo taking advantage... I don't want the same thing happening to any1 else so m telling every1...

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Very convenient mall with many food and shopping options all in one place. Easy access because it’s connected directly to Punggol MRT. For anyone looking for the musollah, it’s located at B2 near NTUC and hidden beside the lift door.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The last time I visited this mall was a few years back. I went for a fund raising walk when centre first opened . So much has changed since then and the place has grown with so many new interesting food outlets. New residents that move into this estate are blessed with such a modern hangout place with everything under one roof. Great food, sports facility, shopping variety covering personal care , home improvement, pet grooming and cinema. Two wings East n West are loaded with 3 floors of shops to pamper oneself and with 2 podium hosting events . One is so pampered, no more need to go downtown as everything is at your doorstep. place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
This mall has everything you need for a relaxing weekend. There is a kids’ play area, as well as a jogging track with a beautiful lake view.

It offers plenty of dining options, including Din Tai Fung, Song Fa Bak Kut Teh, and many others. For tea lovers, there are popular choices such as CHICHA San Chen and CHAGEE. If you’re looking for dessert, Henri Charpentier and Mister Donut are great options. Coffee lovers can also grab a drink at Starbucks.

The mall also has a supermarket, making it a convenient one-stop destination for shopping, dining, and leisure. It’s a perfect place to spend the weekend, especially if you prefer not to travel to the city centre.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Waterway Point is a large and spacious mall with a good mix of shops, products, services and plenty of food options. It covers most day-to-day needs and works well as a one-stop destination for residents in the area. That said, it gets very crowded on weekends, so be prepared for heavy foot traffic. On the plus side, there’s ample parking available, which helps. Overall, a solid and well-planned mall — just better enjoyed during off-peak hours.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Waterway point shopping mall shopping I would say clothing shops like Uniqlo n playdress.there is a popular bookstore n a toys r us.there is a miniso ...Japan home n value shop I would not really put it in a shopping category... More of home maybe ... waterway point for shopping not exactly a place .... For food I would not say that I'm so satisfied with waterway point's food options .... There r foods like fast foods like McDonalds, Jollibee,mos burger, n there r foods like kaya toast n munching snacks like old chang kee n fried banana.there is a foodcourt within the mall but NOT HAPPY WITH IT.there r foods like fish soup n yong Tau foo but there is no Indian food or Muslim food.there is a western stall in the foodcourt but I don't find much difference in eating fast foods n eating a western meal.There r also a Thai food stall which sells fried noodles n fried rice ... This foodcourt mainly Chinese foods ... For a Indian girl like me food options r very limited not only in this food court but I m talking about the whole waterway point mall ... I mean seriously the mall is so big n spacious but there is no any Indian food in it or at least a Muslim food on it ... Very disappointed ☹️ whenever I go waterway point the only options I get to eat is either fast food or Milo toast ...there r restaurants like buddy hoggies n han's ... But seriously if I want to enjoy a nice meal I have to spend so much money??? Can't I get to enjoy a nice meal for a cheaper price at a normal foodcourt???  There r take away shops like stuff'd .... Kebab, burrito, burrito bowl used to cost much cheaper than the price is costing now ... Just increased the price for kebab, burrito n burrito bowls ... N no dine in options from last time till now ... I felt not worth buying from this shop ... But I always c a long queue in this shop ... ☹️ There is a shop which similar like stuff'd...
Guzman y gormez which also sells kebab n burritos ... it has dine in options... BUT VERY EXPENSIVE... have tried there before ... Did not went back to that shop again 🤫 ... Oh n I just remembered... Waterway point has waffles.... For any1 who is a sweet tooth ... 😁 Many flavored drinks like luckin coffee ... N boost ... There r many flavored drinks shops in waterway point but not healthy... Too much sugar... I don't really encourage people to drink all this flavored drinks...
Whatever age anybody is or whether people with diabetes or not too much sugar intake n too much fast foods intake NOT HEALTHY...
Another thing which I want to tell everyone is that when eating at McDonalds please do not give your hp to any1 in the McDonald's... I have this prob with McDonalds whenever I m eating McDonalds... Since I eat at waterway McDonalds today n this prob happened again I wish to tell everyone... The person who is wearing the yellow shirt in the McDonald's I guess she is the manager's assistant she asked for my phone to do McDonalds feedback...I did not want to be rude so gave hery HP ... Customers r supposed to do the feedback not any1 else ... The McDonald's was so dirty ... I had to wipe the table before I eat ... Fly was flying around... I want to feedback but cannot bec she do feedback... The feedback she did from my phone was submitted under my name but I did do the feedback... Just bec they want to increase their outlet rating they do feedback from customers phone ... Everything they put 5 star ... Taking a customer's phone n doing feedback is very wrong ... As a customer I can make a complaint as this has happened many times ... But I just decided to just leave it ... Next time I go McDonalds when again ask for my phone I m strictly going to say as a customer I will do feedback ... I guess I have to be rude at times ... M being too nice ... Peo taking advantage... I don't want the same thing happening to any1 else so m telling every1...

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Very convenient mall with many food and shopping options all in one place. Easy access because it’s connected directly to Punggol MRT. For anyone looking for the musollah, it’s located at B2 near NTUC and hidden beside the lift door.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The last time I visited this mall was a few years back. I went for a fund raising walk when centre first opened . So much has changed since then and the place has grown with so many new interesting food outlets. New residents that move into this estate are blessed with such a modern hangout place with everything under one roof. Great food, sports facility, shopping variety covering personal care , home improvement, pet grooming and cinema. Two wings East n West are loaded with 3 floors of shops to pamper oneself and with 2 podium hosting events . One is so pampered, no more need to go downtown as everything is at your doorstep. A great place for families to spend time.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Extremely NOISY construction works of the depot extension!!! They work 24/7 every day, including weekends. Despite numerous feedback, they still carry on throughout the nights that disrupt our sleep and everybody who stays near this damn construction site. Terrible and inconsiderate LTA!!! Recently the machineries the workers operated emitted fumes that smelled like exhaust fumes that forced me to close all my windows in my house at night so we won't breathe in the fumes. The excessive noises and the toxic fumes from this damn depot extension construction are affecting all who are staying near the site. 😡😡😡

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Modern large bus depot for maintenance of SBS Transit trains. Reachable by two LRT stations Tongkang and Rebjong. Plenty of fasciitis including canteen and groceries. Conveniently located in the neighbourhood hartland two LRT stations to Sengkang MRT station and bus interchange Compass One Kopitium Square with plenty of dinning shopping supermarket banking library postage community centre cinemas entertainment and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for groups expats locals visitors and families.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: A very well maintained underground depot. All of the trains are shining despite operated for years. There is a very well defined protocol inside the depot. Surely SBS is doing much better than SMRT in terms of safety and maintenance.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Plaza Singapura is very crowded but extremely useful. I had multiple skin and hair treatments here and got excellent results was 99.99.9% effective. You can trust the professionals here; they deliver exactly what they promise.😎

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is a long established shopping mall very popular with the locals especially when Yaohan, a Japanese brand first opened up it’s flagship super supermarket here and it was always crowded with families visiting, shopping and dining. It’s sitting immediately on the right when you face the ISTANA.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Sleek and modern mall with immense verticality, spanning up towards the 7th storey. Each floor especially the upper ones are not that large hence it's quite easy to navigate, it is very cool and air-conditioned. Aside from that, the toilets arerelativelyv clean and there is an assortment of food, entertainment, and shopping as well scattered throughout the mall. It is also conveniently linked to the large Dhoby Ghaut MRT Interchange which makes this mall very accessible.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Overall the staff are professional but I think they are not precise. More details below

Called Passion Card hotline to get refund on lost passion card on behalf and is told to go cc. So I called the cc to confirm before I went down. After filling the form, I left and I call back to told them that I have tick the wrong bank. It was then that they told me the card lost is silver passion card and i had to go down to hougang before tearing my form.

I went home and realize the card is not a silver card. I called them and told them that they gave the wrong info and had already tear my form. So they ask to me come down again.

They are professional throughout but seems like they are not clear of the process and not able to say no. I had told them that if it is possible to get the refund in my case and they use professional terms like they can't guarantee I can get back the money, which is not helpful. I had to use the right terms like is it administrative possible to process such case and I understand money refund is not guarantee. I even had to mention that it is okay if they are not sure as I can escalate it with the passion hotline. They still insist I come down and confirm it is possible. To be fair to them, I mention again if they not sure is okay but if I had to come down and it is administrative not possible to process I will escalate.

So I made another trip down and they mention that I can't sign on behalf and have to bring back the form.

I gave up and do it at Rivervale cc. You may refer to my review there. The experience is better and I even got an acknowledgement slip.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Lousy service. Well I already called to inform and apologise that I am late to collect my HPB tracker on behalf of my family member and this person said can come now and take at singpost level 3. And so I rushed here. And in the end it's all closed.

Please know your stuff well to serve the public better and if I could suggest, please stop all this collection with certain timing.. Just do self service collect parcel since it's 24hrs. You only burden people like this. Please report this to MP or whoever in power to improve this.

Thank you and I look forward for this change.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Sengkang Community Club, more commonly know as Sengkang CC is located fairly close to the Sengkang General Hospital and even closer to Compass One(previously known as Compass Point).

Foreigners or people new to the place may not have a clue to the purpose of the building as it does not host the common restaurants or businesses.  The signages  there do not stand out either. However, locals know it to be a building of necessary amenities.

There is no ColdStorage, nor Fairprice or even a bubble tea shop here. I have only ever seen just one standard shop or restaurant by the name of Uncle Toby's Tuckshop. It serves food, beverages and patrons there have a simple  outdoor seating area to enjoy their snacks or meals. The only period of time where there will be a lack of customers outside on the seats is in the afternoons, where the sun heats the seats up to a rear-end scorching temperature.

The Sengkang CC building hosts  the Anti-Narcotics Association office, a neighbourhood police centre, a SingHealth Polyclinic, a women's clinic,  Singapore Post postal service and a People's Association CC. There are of course others such as the gym, tuition centres, a multipurpose hall and a basketball court situated outside.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Just attended CNY 2024 dinner here. The food is nice. Multi entertainment. Really enjoyed. Looking forward to 2025. Good Job for the volunteers too. Thanks for the coordination for us to enjoyed the program. Plus lucky draw too

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: ADLINA is the best! Thank you for your exceptional customer service. First time to visit this clinic as I am suffering from extreme lower back pain & I need an urgent medication. I am so lucky that she's the one who assisted me in my inquiries and she even advise me to go to TCM and my back pain was resolved through therapy/massage instantly. If it's not for her recommendation, I maybe sick until now. Thank you so much 🙏🏻

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
✔️ Review of Hougang Mall
90 Hougang Ave 10, Singapore
538766

💙💙💙 I'm excited to share that I was selected as the winner of the Water Wally & Sally Plushie giveaway for Singapore's World Water Day 2026! To claim my prize, I visited Hougang Mall, which gave me the perfect opportunity to revisit and explore the area, bringing back cherished childhood memories.

Having recently moved to Tampines, I traveled to Hougang Mall, which took about an hour. The trip was a nostalgic journey back to my younger days when I often shopped there.

Hougang Mall provides a wide selection of stores that cater to the needs of most local residents.

It houses numerous shops and recently opened Food & Beverage outlets, such as NTUC FairPrice, BreakTalk, a food court, various restaurants, pharmacies, an electronics retailer (Harvey Norman), jewelry stores, beauty salons, spas, hair salons, and apparel shops.

The mall tends to be busy daily and can become quite crowded during weekends. It also regularly hosts events and promotional activities.

With its inviting ambiance and friendly staff, shopping at Hougang Mall offers a pleasant experience.

It is a must-visit destination for those nearby seeking convenient and diverse shopping options—a great place to unwind and refresh.

Visit Hougang Mall, a true Heartland Mall, and enjoy a fun overall experience.

🙂 All essential items can be found here without the need to travel to the city.

Chris
17 April 2026

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Nice neighborhood mall with almost everything you need. Food options are plenty and the prices are quite reasonable, which is always a big plus.

The air conditioning is also very good. Very important, because shopping without good AC is basically cardio.

Only downside is that it does not connect directly to the MRT station. That would make it much more convenient.

Overall, a good mall for daily needs, food and a comfortable break from the heat.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Newly Upgraded Hougang Mall with many New F&B Outlets. Spoilt for choice, variety Authentic European & SEA Food Stalls here. Vibrant & POSITIVE ENERGY ENVIRONMENT Shopping Mall here. Can't wait for the New Renovated Hougang Library at Level 4 coming June/July...hopefully.
There is Value Shop & other Reasonable priced groceries & snacks.
Entertainment Game Station, NTUC Fair Price, BreakTalk, Wok Hey, Pet Lovers, Pharmacies, Jewellery shop, Beauty, Facial, Nail polish, Spa & Hair Salon.
Harvey Norman, Data Shoes & other brands. Enjoy your One Stop North East Hougang Central HG Shopping Mall here.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Hougang Mall is a vibrant shopping destination in Singapore, offering a mix of local and international brands. The mall's cozy atmosphere and friendly staff make shopping a pleasant experience. With various dining options, including popular food courts and cafes, it's a great place to relax and refuel. The mall also hosts regular events and promotions, adding to its appeal. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall experience is enjoyable. A must-visit for anyone in the area looking for a convenient and diverse shopping experience

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The new renovated mall definitely gives it a fresh look! However, I don't understand why they shut down the escalator from level 1 to level 3. I see elderly folks struggling to climb up and down. For those of us who can walk, it's not a big deal. I see people who are able-bodied waiting for the already small lift, while people who really need it are waiting too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Visited in the late morning before the weather got too hot , historical park with heritage buildings & a gallery of history of this place was event fully refreshing .

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Sengkang MRT/LRT is super convenient — everything you need is connected in one spot. Whether I’m heading to work, grabbing groceries at Compass One, or just getting around the neighborhood, this station makes it easy.

The transfers between MRT and LRT are seamless, and it’s well-integrated with the mall, bus interchange, and even food options nearby. The place is generally clean, organized, and easy to navigate, even during peak hours.

I pass through here almost daily, and I rarely have issues. Trains come on time, announcements are clear, and the whole station feels safe and efficient. One of the better hubs in the northeast, for sure.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Best place for crowds. Weekday and weekend peak hour full of crowd. This place is the place to be if you like to squeeze, enjoy the body contact and stress.  Sometimes breast to breast. Next stop to look out after here is Serangoon. Same situation, but with interchange to CC line and so on.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Hainanese curry rice: Fragrant curry, good portion (especially with the rice)
Mixed veg rice: Often a long queue in the evening. They have good fried rice, about $5+ for 1 veg+1 meat+fried rice. Ingredients are nice
Herbal soup: Average portion, nice lotus root soup (though, it tastes about the same in most herbal soup stalls), goes well with pumpkin rice.
Korean: Both Korean stalls are fine, Jin Kimchi in particular is a chain stall but the quality is good and the packaging is well sealed.
Butter and Cream: really good cakes and tarts, but I recommend eating fresh. Especially the egg tarts, they taste much better fresh than kept overnight in the fridge.
Carrot cake: white carrot cake is pretty good, but can be quite salty / oily. They forgot to add / remove chili based on request a few times...but overall the food is good and I will buy again
Drink stall: Teh peng is ok. Avocado drink is a scam, the gula melaka is very sweet and tastes good but I am fairly certain "avocado juice" should be blended...not have the whole avocado....

There's nothing in particular that stands out a lot, but it's relatively affordable in general and food is not bad! Just won't recommend the avocado "juice" 😹

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Big, convenient hawker centre near Sengkang MRT & Compass One offering a wide variety of local & international food at reasonable prices. Seating is spacious & breezy after renovations.

10% off when paying via the FairPrice app 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This 1 star is given to a Vietnamese Bánh mì stall.
It cost me SG$8 and the piece of chicken inside can't even been seen outside the bread. It's smaller than 旺旺米饼. When the lady pass the packet of Bánh mì to me, its feels like I am holding a empty bag. Don't waste your money on this stall. For SG$8, I can have a big piece of fried chicken for Economic Rice with another meat and vegi, a big burger, a 2 pieces fried chicken from western food stall, a whole chicken from Cold Storage with $0.10 less.
Photo shows that I have eaten half of the bread and the chicken is haven't appear yet.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Dear Owner of Kopitiam(Fairprice Group), may a humble common peasant like me ask, have your company staffs trained to handle the drinks stall with the standard operation procedure through out all the kopitiam outlet. If so, how do you justify the amount of drinks that is served to customer. $2.70 for this cup of milo, a cup full of ice (to the brim) and poured in with cold (ready made) milo, compared to outlets that serve the same thing, a cup full of ice and poured in with hot(freshly made) milo. And also given the recent inflation, does Fairprice Group find this price ($2.70) reasonable? Thank you.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: The stall that sells curry rice v generous. Can choose veggie ☺️. Prev tried the chicken chop. Today tried the fish fillet

Mixed veg rice: Often a long queue in the evening. They have good fried rice, about $5+ for 1 veg+1 meat+fried rice. Ingredients are nice
Herbal soup: Average portion, nice lotus root soup (though, it tastes about the same in most herbal soup stalls), goes well with pumpkin rice.
Korean: Both Korean stalls are fine, Jin Kimchi in particular is a chain stall but the quality is good and the packaging is well sealed.
Butter and Cream: really good cakes and tarts, but I recommend eating fresh. Especially the egg tarts, they taste much better fresh than kept overnight in the fridge.
Carrot cake: white carrot cake is pretty good, but can be quite salty / oily. They forgot to add / remove chili based on request a few times...but overall the food is good and I will buy again
Drink stall: Teh peng is ok. Avocado drink is a scam, the gula melaka is very sweet and tastes good but I am fairly certain "avocado juice" should be blended...not have the whole avocado....

There's nothing in particular that stands out a lot, but it's relatively affordable in general and food is not bad! Just won't recommend the avocado "juice" 😹

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Big, convenient hawker centre near Sengkang MRT & Compass One offering a wide variety of local & international food at reasonable prices. Seating is spacious & breezy after renovations.

10% off when paying via the FairPrice app 👍

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This 1 star is given to a Vietnamese Bánh mì stall.
It cost me SG$8 and the piece of chicken inside can't even been seen outside the bread. It's smaller than 旺旺米饼. When the lady pass the packet of Bánh mì to me, its feels like I am holding a empty bag. Don't waste your money on this stall. For SG$8, I can have a big piece of fried chicken for Economic Rice with another meat and vegi, a big burger, a 2 pieces fried chicken from western food stall, a whole chicken from Cold Storage with $0.10 less.
Photo shows that I have eaten half of the bread and the chicken is haven't appear yet.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Dear Owner of Kopitiam(Fairprice Group), may a humble common peasant like me ask, have your company staffs trained to handle the drinks stall with the standard operation procedure through out all the kopitiam outlet. If so, how do you justify the amount of drinks that is served to customer. $2.70 for this cup of milo, a cup full of ice (to the brim) and poured in with cold (ready made) milo, compared to outlets that serve the same thing, a cup full of ice and poured in with hot(freshly made) milo. And also given the recent inflation, does Fairprice Group find this price ($2.70) reasonable? Thank you.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: The stall that sells curry rice v generous. Can choose veggie ☺️. Prev tried the chicken chop. Today tried the fish fillet

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I want to express my gratitude to Skool4kidz Sengkang Riverside Park for providing a secure and safe environment for our child Nova Zehraa. Thank you to all PG teachers for educating, comforting, loving and nurturing Nova Zehraa. She enter there, she started in PG class. It’s Nova first time being away from us, so im so glad that Nova can really adjust to the place. Now Nova has promoted to N1D. As a mother, my top priority has always been my child’s happiness, health, and security, and finding this at your preschool is reassuring. Due to all the consistent guidance at school, Nova is now quite an independent child and is able to do some things on her own. She knows what to pack in her bag when going to school. Earlier was hard to settled Nova every morning when entering the school because of separation anxiety with us, but the teachers really know how to win Nova heart and comfort her till she is adjusted to it. Going home time, Nova will always cry upon us fetching her, but now Nova will show us happy face n smile, hug her teachers n wave goodbye. Nova often tells me that she enjoys the school, in a way of her sings and dance at home whenever I ask her how school is. Although she can’t talk much yet, but her movements and the way she try to explain to us, makes me think that she really loves the school curriculum. Teacher Lestly & Han Laoshi invested time and effort in planning engaging lesson activities and meticulously documented their observations for our child: These portfolios kept us well-informed about their progress in school. I was happy reading them. I know Nova had a very great, fun & loving teachers at school. Thank you Teacher Lestly & Han Laoshi.
HUGS! ❤️❤️❤️❤️

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Had a really pleasant experience visiting the school today. Sharon, who helped us with the registration, was extremely detailed and patient in explaining everything to us.

Principal Reina also gave us a full tour of the school and explained the setup and design of the facilities very thoughtfully. We really appreciated how she shared the reasons behind the school’s environment and learning spaces, as well as the strong emphasis placed on maintaining quality through continuous teacher and principal training every year.

The whole visit felt very professional, warm, and reassuring. Thank you Sharon and Principal Reina for taking the time to make us feel so welcomed and informed!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My wife and I recently visited the preschool for a school tour with our 2-year-old daughter, and we were genuinely very impressed with the overall experience from start to finish.

One of the biggest highlights of the school is definitely its unique and independent location within Sengkang Riverside Park. Unlike many preschools located at the void decks of HDB blocks, the school offers a much greater sense of privacy, security, and exclusivity. The surrounding greenery, peaceful atmosphere, and fresh air create a very pleasant and healthy environment for children to grow, learn, and play in.

The school environment itself felt extremely welcoming and conducive for early childhood education. The classrooms and play areas were clean, well-organised, and thoughtfully planned out for young children. We were also very happy to learn that the school prepares meals through its own in-house kitchen instead of relying on external catering services, which gave us additional confidence and assurance as parents.

Another aspect that stood out strongly was the professionalism and friendliness of everyone we met during the visit. From the teachers and administrative staff to even the security guard at the guard house, every single person greeted us warmly and made us feel comfortable and welcomed throughout the tour.

We would especially like to express our sincere appreciation to Senior Principal Reina, who personally conducted the tour for us. She was incredibly patient, detailed, and knowledgeable throughout the session. As first-time parents preparing to send our daughter to school for the first time, we naturally had many worries and concerns regarding separation anxiety, safety, routines, and how our child would adapt to the new environment. Reina showed great empathy and understanding towards our concerns and took the time to address every question carefully. She explained the school’s daily routines, programmes, learning approach, outdoor activities, and safety measures very clearly and comprehensively. Her genuine care and passion for early childhood education were very evident, and she made us feel much more reassured and confident after the tour.

We would also like to specially thank Customer Service Officer Reena for her professionalism and patience in explaining all the administrative and financial matters. She provided a very clear breakdown of the school fees, subsidies available, enrolment procedures, first-day requirements, as well as the terms and conditions. Everything was explained thoroughly and transparently, which we greatly appreciated.

Overall, the school left us with an extremely positive impression. The combination of a beautiful green environment, caring and professional staff, quality facilities, and a strong sense of warmth and safety truly makes this preschool stand out. We are very thankful for the wonderful hospitality and informative experience throughout the tour.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We really enjoyed our visit to the school. Principal Reina was very warm and welcoming, and took the time to personally show us around the classrooms from Nursery to K2. It gave us a clear picture of the environment and how the children learn and interact each day.

Throughout the tour, everyone we met — from the security staff to the teachers and admin team (especially Sharon)— was friendly and approachable, which made us feel very comfortable.

Overall, the school left us with a strong impression of being a safe, caring, and nurturing place for young children. As parents, that sense of warmth and reassurance means a lot to us. We truly appreciate the time and effort the team put into hosting us.

Didn’t take long for me to register my child in this school after the tour :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We had a wonderful experience with Skool4kidz at Sengkang Riverside Park, and I highly recommend it for infants and toddlers.
The teachers are exceptionally responsible, dedicated, and knowledgeable. What truly stood out to us was their attentiveness to the children’s well-being. On several occasions, they were able to pick up early signs that our son might be unwell—even before symptoms like wheezing cough or fever became apparent. Their proactive communication allowed us to take timely action, and we are very grateful for that level of care and vigilance.
The teachers and staff are also consistently warm, friendly, and welcoming. Every drop-off and pick-up was a pleasant experience, which gave us great reassurance as parents.
We also appreciate the school environment. Being located next to Sengkang Riverside Park provides a pleasant and refreshing setting for the children.
Our son spent 17 months in infant care and 1 month in playgroup here before we had to transfer him to another school closer to our workplace. Even during the transition, the school was very supportive—they kindly loaned us a playgroup uniform at no cost, which we truly appreciated.
A special thank you to Teacher Siti, Teacher Evi, and Admin Sharon for their care and dedication. Your efforts have made a meaningful difference in our child’s early years.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We recently took our little one to Sengkang Public Library, which is conveniently located right inside Compass One. It made for a really easy family outing, as we could grab a quick bite at the mall before heading up. The library itself is quite spacious and felt very clean and well-maintained, spread across two levels. Our child absolutely loved the Early Literacy Area; they have some really engaging displays and the book modules, designed like traditional fishing baskets, were a nice touch that caught our attention. It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

There is also a quick charge area. Near the quick charge area there are a few shelves of adult fiction.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: This is a two storey library and that easily makes it better. If you feel like the children section can be loud, you may find other reading spots. Conveniently located inside the mall with lots of reading books for small children. Lots of library staff too to remind the children to keep quiet while inside the library. My daughters love to hang out in this library after school.

It’s a bright and welcoming space, perfect for little hands to explore.

While the children's section can get a bit lively, as expected with so many excited kids, there are plenty of other quiet spots throughout the library if you need a calmer environment. My wife and I appreciated the wide selection of books for all ages, and I even spotted a dedicated 'Tween Space' which will be great for our child in a few years. There are ample seating areas, so it's easy to settle down with a stack of books and spend a good hour or two. It’s definitely a solid choice for a family afternoon out, combining learning with comfort.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:

The first thing I noticed that drew my attention when I walked in was this little Harry Potter castle, which was on top of a bookshelf. The second thing I noticed was that they had a huge selection of audiovisuals.

Once you walk a bit more towards the left, you’ll see a few bookshelves, and close to those bookshelves is a long white table. And near the white table there are 2 seats close to a window. I think that area is a really nice spot for reading, studying, and using your computers.

If you want straight, you’ll see comics, and a teen section. In between 2 bookshelves, there are 2 seats, for safe distancing. There is also a magazine section.

Overall, I think the Sengkang Library is a nice library for spending a long duration of time with your computer, or having a book you want to read. You can also study in the area of the white table and 2 seats near the window.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
This is very great neighbourhood library with librarians who are very observant and go the extra mile. Especially I thank Ms Fazilah 9 Nov 2025 at 7.30pm. She goes the extra mile fuss free, efficient, responsive and responsible. She checked the return conveyor belt and took effort to accompany me downstairs to search shelf and all displays thoroughly. She is very detailed to get both call number and shelf number. She found the book at display within very short time and went back to her routine duty which impresses me with her work ethics.

Before Ms Fazilah helped me I tried to get the book as search shown available on shelf but cannot find. I approached another white hair lady who situated there. She gave up very fast after just finding from the usual numbered shelf which I told her I already done. Then just quickly dismissed me and gave me excuses impatiently that it may be on wrong shelf. This is although I explained I am very sure it should be somewhere in library because not many people in library and I saw no one using the book. So the previous staff did not help me much she did not even look at special display areas...

Thank you so much Sengkang library for having MS Fazilah such a rare, helpful exemplary staff who exhibits great time management and problem solving skill. I thank her for resolving patiently and quickly without excuse and not dismissing customer request.
