place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
You can always find some hidden gems there! Basically, if we see a stall with a long queue… we just try it 😂

🍗 Hainanese Chicken Rice — We love chicken rice! The chicken was okay, some parts were a bit dry, but still satisfying as our first chicken rice in Singapore this year.

🍜 Mixed Hor Fun — Loved it with a mix of meats and seafood! Not a big fan of the burnt parts, but overall still enjoyable.

🥤 Sugar Cane Juice — So refreshing, especially in the hot weat

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Visit there on a Saturday morning about 9am. Most of the stalls are not operating yet. However we found 2 stalls that gives real value for money. The wanton mee stall ( facing the blocks behind) served a sumptuous bowl of noodles with 4 wantons and a generous helping of char siew at $4. Have eaten at other hawker centres I have not seen such portions. The prawn noodles is even better. Their normal sized prawns were so big it's like a big prawns and it's only $4.50. Worth another view.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
0 star for the char kway tiao and hokkien mee(#01-13)! They are terrible! No fragrance just full of salty burnt taste. Yucks.

The dumplings were decent.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Stopped by Ding Sheng, unit #01-78. Great service by this 'uncle'. Ordered Mui Fan, $5.50. Average, nothing exceptional. Tried Aunty Jo's dessert, ice jelly with sea coconut, $2.80. This place gets a little crowded from 7pm onwards. Also try Hane Chiang Thai food , #01-21. Tom Yum soup is ok.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: The Thunder Tea Story. Came to AV food centre for lunch and am glad to find a healthy food option. Affordable at $5.50 (option for white or brown rice) and extra charge for any add-on option . I added Chai Por Neng (Egg with Radish) for some protein at extra $1.50.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This hawker center was the first place I visited right after I landed in Singapore. Personally, I cannot say that this was the best in Singapore since I have not yet tried other places at the time.

As a first time traveler, Newton Hawkers were so welcoming! I had a great time with the vibes by the uncles and aunties offering their products to me. Majority of the crowd are also local, which is a plus for me. This specific stall (in the picture) was the best (service-wise).

In the photo, I have made friends with Auntie Nico and Uncle John. I don’t know their relationship or if they own the store but they treated me so well and even offered to show me how they cook their food (I told them that I own a restaurant and was curious about how they cook their food).

In the end of the night, I asked them to take a picture with me and they did. Auntie Nico also used her phone to get a selfie with me lol. Fun times!

There’s always something beautiful about hard working people and everytime you get a chance to talk to them, do it!

WILL ALWAYS VISIT HERE EVERYTIME I GO TO SINGAPORE!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Newton Food Centre is a must-visit in Singapore. Lively, authentic, and packed with incredible food options, it’s everything you want from a classic hawker centre.

The variety is outstanding — from local favourites to late-night comfort dishes, there’s something for everyone. Prices are very reasonable, portions are generous, and the flavours are bold and authentic. It’s the kind of place where you can try multiple dishes in one visit and still want to come back again.

The atmosphere is busy and energetic, especially in the evenings, but that’s part of the charm. Despite the crowds, there’s plenty of seating and the space is well laid out. Being open 24 hours is a huge bonus, whether you’re after a late supper, early breakfast, or anything in between.

Great spot for travellers wanting a genuine local experience, groups with different tastes, or anyone keen to eat well without breaking the bank. Casual, vibrant, and full of character — exactly what a Singapore hawker centre should be.

🍜 Huge variety of food
🌶️ Authentic local flavours
💰 Affordable prices
⏰ Open 24 hours
🎉 Lively atmosphere

Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
• Food
The food selection at this center is a reliable choice for local flavors. The chicken wings and satay are particular highlights, especially when paired with a cold beverage. Another notable mention is the Gong Gong, which is a dish that often prompts a visit to this location. The flavors are authentic and provide a satisfying taste of local seafood and grilled items.

• Service & Atmosphere
The atmosphere is lively and typical of a popular open-air food center in Singapore. It offers a vibrant setting that is suitable for casual gatherings. While the environment can get busy and crowded, it remains a practical and energetic spot for a late-night meal or a social drink. The service varies by stall but is generally efficient and functional.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Well known hawker centre that once upon a time catered mostly to tourists.

But locals are now crowding the place because of the good food and the variety and because the Singapore Tourism Board has successfully clamped down some seafood stalls touting and scamming patrons, especially tourists, with exorbitant prices.

This is one hawker centre where you will find people from all over the world savouring local street food of all races in Singapore which originated from China, India,  Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,  etc and even the US.

Beer by the jugs are served here.

Most stalls are closed for lunch. But it is usually quite difficult to get a table during dinner time, especially on weekends. Sharing table is the norm.  So be willing to do so.

Queuing at some stalls to collect your own order is also the norm.

This hawker centre, including the free toilets, are very clean and airy. There are basins all over for patrons to wash their hands before and after meals
meals.

Pay when you order. In cash or via your phone transfer.

There is an adjoining carpark. A huge one. But it can still be hard to find a parking space during dinner time.

The Newton MRT is very near by.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A true foodie paradise, with flavors from across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and China all coming together in one place. You’ll find yourself eating alongside both locals and tourists, which adds to the energy and authenticity. Not too crowded on a Monday night, making it easy to explore. Highlights included the fresh sugar cane juice, black pepper crab, Singapore chili stingray, fried squid, and the classic Singapore “carrot cake.” A great way to experience the diversity of the region’s street food culture.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

Timbre+ One North is a great dining destination in Singapore's One North district, offering a vibrant atmosphere that appeals to a wide range of visitors. Whether you are meeting colleagues after work, catching up with friends, hosting a casual business discussion, or simply looking for a good meal, it provides a welcoming and comfortable setting.

One of its biggest strengths is the variety of food available. With options ranging from Indian and Chinese cuisine to continental dishes and local favourites, there is something to suit almost every taste and preference. This makes it an excellent choice for groups with diverse food interests.

The venue is clean, well organized, and efficiently managed, creating a pleasant dining experience even during busy periods. The lively yet relaxed environment makes it suitable for both working lunches and social gatherings.

Good food, reasonable prices, and a wide selection of cuisines make Timbre+ One North a place that offers excellent value for money and is well worth a visit when in the area.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
It’s like hawker food but abit pricy by $1-2

Variety is limited imo. Theres a Thailand, Malay, Chinese, Korean and some food trucks do burgers and fried chicken.

Food is decent but not exactly wow. The space is huge and many seating area, I’d assume it’ll be crowded during lunch hours from the offices nearby

Ambience is lit.. there is a live band too, but it’s pretty loud and hurts the ear. Good if you prefer not to hear each other in a conversation. A lot of alcohol people there, more to chill and enjoy the music.

Interesting vibes but not for everyone

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Open air food court with live band on Friday evening. A little too noisy for me but can see the crowd were enjoying themselves much and with very good vibes. Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Nice hawker scene near one-north mrt. Good variety of food with some modern healthy options to cater to the younger working demographics. Interesting beer taps, an option for day drinking

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Found this pancake eating place,  looks very nice, and usually pancake dish like this unable to find in HDB state, even restaurant don’t sell like this.  Will give a try next round when we re- visit this place.  The shop is just opp if Nanyang Poly main gate.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 1 Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
My son enjoy going to the student care very much. The teachers here are very patient and caring and genuinely care for the children’s well being.
The daily schedule is well-balanced — children have dedicated homework time, enrichment activities, and opportunities for rest and play. The environment is clean, safe, and welcoming. My child especially enjoys the holiday programmes and themed activities, which are both educational and fun.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 1 Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: My daughter was having challenge in some subjects at school, we explored few tuition options and decided to choose and start with EduFirst Learning Centre Fernvale in July. The teachers have been very patient and helpful in guiding her, within short period of time, she rebuilt her confidence in these subjects and I saw the improvement in her. I recommend this centre as the environment is conducive and teachers are engaging and committed.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 1 Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: I'm thoroughly impressed with this learning centre! The staff are knowledgeable, supportive, and genuinely care about their students' success. The environment is welcoming and conducive to learning. I've seen significant improvement in my understanding and grades since joining. I highly recommend this centre to anyone looking for a nurturing and effective learning experience.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 1 Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: My son is attending EduFirst@fernvale for the student care service and just started on English tuition. The teachers are attentive and would highlight to us as and when they find that our child is not feeling well. The school holiday programme arranged are engaging and our child looks forward to go for the activities everyday during school holiday period. The teachers also guided him to do past year papers and assessment books during the after school hours even before we started him on English tuition there and we see progress specifically in his English and Chinese. We can see that although the teachers allocated free time for the kids to play, they are also strict in ensuring that they do not misbehave.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Sengkang 1 Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: At Primary 6, I noticed that my daughter struggled with mathematics as she had problem answering various problem sums and she had just passed for the mid year exam paper, which is exceptionally lower than her expectations. She wanted help in pulling her grades back up for Prelims and Psle, thus she approached me, requesting me to help her find a tuition centre where she can improve on her maths. After searching through multiple tuition centres, I collated multiple options for her to choose, which ultimately, she decided to choose EduFirst Learning Centre (Fernvale). This was a decision she did not regret whatsoever. When she first joined, the lessons were hosted on zoom due to COVID-19. Although she was a newly-joined student, the current students and teachers welcomed her with open arms. Despite facing multiple challenges regarding online learning, she persevered though and she also found the lessons with the teacher engaging, fun and interactive. After a few weeks, the COVID-19 safety measurements were lightened and students could return to their respective tuition centres. That was her first time meeting face-to-face with her teacher. She felt a little nervous at first, but it did not take her long to adapt to the new environment. So not long after, it was time for Prelims examinations. With all the help and support she received from her teachers and friends, she managed to meet her target for the exams. She was super elated and that gave her motivation to strive and work harder for Psle to score even higher marks. By psle, she had achieved her goal, and even got 1 grade higher than the desired goal! She was super happy and she was grateful for all the help and support she received. Overall, all the teachers at the centre are very professional and engaging. They are also friendly towards their students and the best thing is about the own time own pace method they use where the students can work on their work themselves and if they have any questions, they can simply ask the teacher so that they can guide the students.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
• Free entry & hours: Compass One is a public shopping mall, so entry is free. The mall’s main contact page lists opening hours as 10:00am–10:00pm daily, while individual stores may open earlier or close later depending on the tenant.

• What & why go: Compass One is a major Sengkang town-centre mall with 180+ brands and a wide mix of shopping, dining and everyday services. It is described by the mall as one of the area’s most extensive and fully integrated malls.

• Top features to see: You’ll find a mix of fashion, food, beauty, electronics and family-friendly outlets, plus anchors like Cold Storage, McDonald’s, Starbucks, POPULAR and the Sengkang Public Library inside the mall.

• Getting there: The mall is at 1 Sengkang Square, Singapore 545078. The official “Getting Here” page says to take the North East Line and alight at Sengkang Station (NE16); buses also serve the mall.

• Parking & practical access: Compass One has on-site parking with published hours for cars and motorcycles. The parking page lists operating hours as 7:00am–11:00pm daily.

• Best for quick visits & family stops: With groceries, food, library access and many daily-need stores, it works well for a short stop, errands, or a family meal after Sengkang Station.

---

⚠️ What to keep in mind

• Store hours vary: Not every shop follows the mall’s main opening hours; some open much earlier for breakfast or close sooner on holidays. Check the tenant page if you are going for a specific store.
• Holiday changes: The mall publishes festive operating hours and some stores may close or shift timings during major holidays.
• Parking charges apply: Parking is paid and has no grace period, so short visits can still incur a charge.
• Facilities are useful but simple: The mall offers customer service on Level 3 and free Wi-Fi with membership perks, but it is still mainly a neighbourhood mall rather than a luxury destination.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Compass 1 shopping mall linked directly to mrt station n Lrt station... Lots to shop like miniso, clothing shops,Japan home,value shop ... Lots to eat ... Kopitiam with wide range of food options from halal to non halal ... Food for everyone to eat ... Fast food options such as McDonalds,kfc, burger king,mos burger,popoyes ... All at compass 1 ...
Bun shops,cakes,old change kee,Mr bean also at compass 1 ... There is a ananas food shop at just outside the entrance to compass 1 which has dine in options... Also a Muslim shop right beside it which is a takeaway place ... Boost,fruit juice shop,pizza,stuff'd and lots more food at compass 1 ... Weekdays r usually less crowded compared to weekends...

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: **Compass One** is a well-planned neighbourhood mall directly linked to Sengkang MRT and bus interchange. Designed by **DCA Architects**, the mall features a clean, contemporary layout with good natural light and clear circulation. Practical mix of shops, dining, and community spaces makes it convenient and comfortable for daily use.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: First time today at this Compass One mall in Sengkang area and quite enjoyed it! Love that it is not as crowded as some of the other heartland malls so we were able to get a carpark without much waiting. Restaurants are not overcrowded too so it is easy to walk with children. Lots of shops too. Thank you for this experience! 🙏🏻
